why_it_matters: A recommendation to replace flow solvers is much riskier than a recommendation to use RL for online candidate selection or warm starts.
next_question: Specify the target workload and decide whether RL is replacing optimization or feeding it.

topic: Optimality and feasibility versus fast adaptation
why_it_matters: Static flow problems reward exactness and certificates, while online traffic engineering may reward low-latency decisions under changing demand.
next_question: Classify the workload as offline planning, online TE, or incident response before selecting a method.

why_it_matters: Promising benchmark results do not automatically transfer to production if traffic, topology, and failures differ from training/evaluation.
next_question: Run shadow-mode replay on real traffic matrices and failure scenarios against the incumbent optimizer.

why_it_matters: Even if RL improves speed or utilization, hard constraints and failure safety can dominate the decision.
next_question: Define a shielded architecture and quantify the residual incident risk versus measured performance gain.

why_it_matters: The same RL system can be unjustified overhead in stable planning and valuable in high-frequency online control.
next_question: Estimate engineering cost, simulator cost, traffic volatility, decision frequency, and value per avoided congestion event.
